baseline.reason: Correctly identifies the off-by-one memset (number_threads vs number_threads+1) as root cause, but the trigger path is wrong: it points to the normal cleanup at line 1199, whereas the bug only manifests when an allocation fails (early return at line 951). In the normal path all elements including weights[number_threads] are allocated by the loop.

vastdb.reason: Correctly identifies the off-by-one memset root cause and accurately pins the trigger path to the early-return error path inside AcquireBilateralTLS when an allocation fails, matching the CVE description ('when the memory allocation fails'). Concise and precise.
